channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Liverpool FC in talks to sell minority stake to Mittal-backed consortium
item.description: The consortium is led by British-Indian investor Amit Bhatia, the son-in-law of Indian steel billionaire Lakshmi Mittal.

item.title: Iranians struggle to get by amid US war and domestic woes
item.description: People are facing mounting pressures, including rising living costs, electricity cuts and bombarded infrastructure.

item.title: Police detain Rahul Gandhi at protest outside PM Modi’s home
item.description: Indian police have detained Congress leader Rahul Gandhi as he protested outside the residence of the Prime Minister.

item.title: Rescuers search for survivors amid risk of fresh floods in Afghanistan
item.description: Death toll rises to at least 23, with more than 100 people missing, as monsoon season spreads chaos across region.

item.title: Where will LeBron James go next? NBA star ‘not rushed’ to make a decision
item.description: Reports have linked James to the Golden State Warriors, the Philadelphia 76ers, the Miami Heat, or the Cleveland Cavaliers.

item.title: Lebanon’s goal to end hostilities with Israel ‘forever’, says president
item.description: Lebanese President Aoun told Trump that Lebanon's “ultimate objective” is to end hostilities with Israel “forever”.

item.title: Salah to Besiktas? Football’s transfer window centre stage after World Cup
item.description: Mohamed Salah's future tops the latest news, contract, salary rumours, and transfer agenda ahead of the new football season.

item.title: Greek-American tourists stabbed near the Acropolis
item.description: Police arrested a 60-year-old man on Tuesday after he allegedly stabbed two Greek-American tourists near the Acropolis.

item.title: An embattled coal terminal could be a lifeline for US coal
item.description: West Oakland residents push legislation to block a coal terminal that could lead to air pollution in their area.

item.title: Trump vows to help Lebanon but offers no timeline for Israeli withdrawal
item.description: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun says his goal is to end 'state of hostility between Lebanon and Israel forever'.

item.title: Police detain Indian opposition leader Gandhi as protests continue to grow
item.description: Detained while picketing PM's residence, Gandhi demands Modi's resignation for 'destroying the future of India's youth'.

item.title: Civilians faced attacks, abuse in joint US-Ecuador operations, HRW says
item.description: Report presents evidence civilian farmers, fisherman were caught up in anti-drug crime operations from January to March.

item.title: External review finds no wrongdoing in Gates Foundation–Epstein links
item.description: Report notes foundation's interaction with US sex offender Epstein on donor fund and grant, but rules out wrongdoing.

item.title: Steep rise in civilian casualties in Russia-Ukraine war, UN says
item.description: Increasing use of long-range missiles and drones against targets far from the front line is worsening the civilian toll.

item.title: US appeals court overturns ruling that freed Columbia activist Mahdawi
item.description: The decision is the latest court victory for US President Donald Trump’s campaign targeting foreign student protesters.

item.title: US eyes new tariffs as existing trade duties near expiration
item.description: US Trade Rep Jamieson Greer hints at new tariffs on 60 trading partners as Trump's temporary tariffs expire on Friday.

item.title: Italy’s Meloni slams protesters following clashes in Bologna
item.description: PM pledges probe into death of Moroccan man under police restraint, condemns violence as protests injure 56 officers.

item.title: UK PM Burnham: ‘We need to be a cost-of-living government’
item.description: At his first cabinet meeting as UK prime minister, Burnham told ministers they must be a "cost-of-living government".

item.title: FIFA ‘destroying football’ for the World Cup, says Spanish football chief
item.description: In a scathing interview, La Liga President Javier Tebas says FIFA chief Gianni Infantino’s time is 'over'.

item.title: Israel has ‘no interest’ in joining US war on Iran, says Smotrich
item.description: Israeli Finance Minister says Israel has "no interest" in joining the renewed US military campaign against Iran.

item.title: Saudi Arabia slams Houthi blockade: How will rest of the world be impacted?
item.description: Saudi Arabia has condemned the Houthi naval blockade that could impact oil flow to importers of Saudi oil.

item.title: Searching for closure: Venezuela families refuse to give up after quakes
item.description: Weeks after earthquakes, relatives are still searching for bodies of loved ones who disappeared beneath the rubble.

item.title: Japan’s AI gamble: Can technology offset the cost of an ageing society?
item.description: From underground networks to emerging AI start-ups, Japan is testing whether its greatest issue can be an opportunity.

item.title: ASEAN diplomats voice ‘serious concern’ over Iran war and energy crisis
item.description: Southeast Asian countries are highly affected by oil supply shortages caused by Iran war and Strait of Hormuz closure.

item.title: What does a heatwave cost?
item.description: What does a heatwave cost?
